items.q: Name the currency used in Malaysia.
items.explanation: The Malaysian ringgit is the currency of Malaysia. Issued by the Central Bank of Malaysia, it is divided into 100 cents.

items.q: What is the capital city of South Australia?
items.explanation: This is a core Australian general-knowledge fact.

items.q: The Andes mountain range is primarily in Africa.
items.explanation: This reflects medium-difficulty geography and civics knowledge.

items.q: What is the official currency of Denmark?
items.explanation: The krone is the official currency of the Kingdom of Denmark, introduced on 1 January 1875. Both the ISO code "DKK" and currency sign "kr." are in common use; the former precedes the value, the latter in some contexts follows it.

items.explanation: This is a core Australian general-knowledge fact.

items.q: What is the capital city of New South Wales?
items.explanation: This is a core Australian general-knowledge fact.

items.q: Which Australian state is abbreviated as QLD?
items.explanation: Queensland is a state in northeastern Australia, the second-largest and third-most populous state in Australia. It is bordered by the Northern Territory, South Australia and New South Wales to the west, south-west and south, respectively.

items.q: What is the capital city of Sweden?
items.explanation: Stockholm is the capital and most populous city of Sweden, as well as the largest urban area in the Nordic countries.

items.q: The UN has six official languages.
items.explanation: This reflects medium-difficulty geography and civics knowledge.
